ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for black sesame filled soft pretzel bun; FOODON_03315021 (pretzel bread product) is the closest defensible anchor. The black sesame paste filling and Japanese sponge-dough method are not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — soft bread rolls
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers soft bread rolls as the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for pretzel-style buns with black sesame filling in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this specialty baked product; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard black sesame soft pretzel bun weighing approximately 100 g, served as a snack or light meal item.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Bread, soft pretzel (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) and Black sesame seeds, adapted composite
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for a black sesame soft pretzel bun made by the Japanese sponge-dough method. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for soft enriched bread rolls (FDC ID 325871) and black sesame seeds (FDC ID 170150), weighted to reflect the described formulation (approximately 80% enriched dough, 20% black sesame paste by weight). Energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, and trans fat values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with this USDA composite estimate and are retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and micronutrient fields not reported in the input are set to -1 where USDA data for the composite is not directly available at this formulation ratio.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — White bread / enriched soft bread roll analogue
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a black sesame soft pretzel bun made by the Japanese sponge-dough method. GI of 72 is estimated using the closest analogous food: white enriched soft bread roll (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008). The black sesame paste filling (approximately 20% by weight) contributes fat and fibre that may modestly lower the effective GI, but this effect is not quantified; the conservative estimate of 72 is retained. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 39.0) / 100 = 28.1 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 280 mg/100 g combined with 12 g added sugar promoting insulin-mediated sodium retention
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bun (approximately 50 g) to keep sodium intake below 140 mg per eating occasion, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods; the 12 g/100 g sugar content may further elevate blood pressure through insulin-driven sodium retention per AHA guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) from refined wheat flour base with 39 g carbohydrate and 12 g sugar per 100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this bun as an occasional food only; if consumed, limit to half a bun (50 g, approximately 19.5 g carbohydrate) and pair with a protein source such as eggs or unsweetened nut butter to blunt the postprandial glucose rise driven by the high-GI refined flour and 12 g/100 g sugar content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.5 g/100 g from butter, trans fat 0.1 g/100 g, and 12 g/100 g added sugar contributing to atherogenic lipid profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit consumption to occasional small portions; the combination of 4.5 g saturated fat, 0.1 g trans fat, and 12 g added sugar per 100 g can contribute to LDL elevation and endothelial inflammation, and total saturated fat intake from all sources should remain below 7% of daily energy per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined wheat flour and 12 g/100 g added sugar promoting pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this bun during active flares, as refined flour and added sugar promote inflammatory cytokine production; the black sesame paste provides some antioxidant lignans but is insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory carbohydrate and sugar burden at this serving size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Black Sesame Soft Pretzel Bun is a Singaporean fusion bakery item that combines the chewy, lye-washed exterior characteristic of a classic soft pretzel with the enriched, pillowy crumb of Japanese-style sweet bread (shokupan-influenced). The dough is prepared using the sponge-dough method — a two-stage fermentation technique that develops a complex flavour and exceptionally tender crumb — and incorporates a scalded flour portion (tangzhong), which gelatinises starch to retain moisture and extend shelf life. The bun is filled with a rich, nutty black sesame paste made from ground roasted black sesame seeds sweetened with sugar, delivering an earthy, slightly bitter, and aromatic contrast to the soft, mildly salty dough. Per 100 g, the bun provides approximately 320 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 14 g fat (4.5 g saturated), 39 g carbohydrate, and 12 g sugar, with 3.5 g dietary fibre and 280 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high GI), making it a high-glycaemic food unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The black sesame filling contributes calcium (100 mg/100 g), iron (1.5 mg/100 g), and sesame lignans with antioxidant properties. Clinically, the combination of refined flour, added sugar, moderate sodium, and saturated fat warrants portion control for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. This product is vegetarian and contains gluten, dairy, eggs, and sesame allergens.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for a black sesame soft pretzel bun made by the Japanese sponge-dough method. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for enriched soft bread rolls and black sesame seeds, weighted to reflect the described formulation. The input record was ai_generated with confidence 0.7; the adapted USDA composite is consistent with the input values, supporting medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white enriched soft bread roll as the closest match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite black sesame pretzel bun formulation is not directly available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy at the specific dough-to-filling ratio used; these values require laboratory analysis of the actual product for precise reporting. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values for the specific composite formulation (80% enriched sponge-dough pretzel bun, 20% black sesame paste) are not directly available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024 for this exact product type. 3. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white enriched soft bread roll (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. 4. The input record listed data_source as 'grabfood' and provenance as 'ai_generated'; per Project Veritas source policy, GrabFood is a forbidden primary source. Nutrient values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central and are consistent; USDA FoodData Central is cited as the approved source. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.1 g/100 g, consistent with trace amounts from dairy butter used in enriched dough; this is a confirmed trace value from the formulation, not a zero. 6. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03315021 and FoodEx2 A000E are approximate matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this specialty product.
